Accessors for a fitted decision-focused lasso

Source: R/fitted-class.R
dfl-accessors.Rd

Read pieces of a DecisionFocusedLasso object without touching its slots. proxy_score(), adaptive_weight(), and penalty_factor() return the per-feature vectors named by feature; selected_features() returns the names the primary fit kept; coverage() returns the coverage report (or NULL for a supplied-regret fit); splits() returns the stored proxy splits; seed() returns the resolved seed; lambda_min()/lambda_1se() return the two standard lambda choices; problem() returns the attached optimisation problem (or NULL).

Usage

proxy_score(object)

# S4 method for class 'DecisionFocusedLasso'
proxy_score(object)

adaptive_weight(object)

# S4 method for class 'DecisionFocusedLasso'
adaptive_weight(object)

penalty_factor(object)

# S4 method for class 'DecisionFocusedLasso'
penalty_factor(object)

selected_features(object)

# S4 method for class 'DecisionFocusedLasso'
selected_features(object)

coverage(object)

# S4 method for class 'DecisionFocusedLasso'
coverage(object)

splits(object)

# S4 method for class 'DecisionFocusedLasso'
splits(object)

seed(object)

# S4 method for class 'DecisionFocusedLasso'
seed(object)

lambda_min(object)

# S4 method for class 'DecisionFocusedLasso'
lambda_min(object)

lambda_1se(object)

# S4 method for class 'DecisionFocusedLasso'
lambda_1se(object)

problem(object)

# S4 method for class 'DecisionFocusedLasso'
problem(object)

Arguments

object

A DecisionFocusedLasso object.

Value

proxy_score(), adaptive_weight(), and penalty_factor() return named numeric vectors. selected_features() returns a character vector. coverage() returns a data frame or NULL. splits() returns a list or NULL. seed() returns a single integer. lambda_min()/lambda_1se() return single numbers. problem() returns an OptimizationProblem object or NULL.

See also

dfl_fit(), DecisionFocusedLasso

Examples

sim <- simulate_capital_allocation(40, 6, 6, seed = 1)
fit <- dfl_fit(
  sim$x, sim$cost, sim$scenario,
  problem = capital_allocation_problem(max_weight = 0.5),
  element_id = sim$element_id,
  control = dfl_control(seed = 1, n_splits = 5L)
)
proxy_score(fit)
#>    feat_01    feat_02    feat_03    feat_04    feat_05    feat_06 
#> 0.47584505 0.27799812 0.07739936 0.28721458 0.17080553 0.11768179 
selected_features(fit)
#> [1] "feat_01" "feat_02" "feat_03" "feat_04"
lambda_min(fit)
#> [1] 0.03070895
